name: Ham and Asparagus Pasta
recipeIngredient: Fresh asparagus spears (3/4 lb) or one 10-oz. package of frozen asparagus cut into 1-inch pieces
Multicolored pasta (10 oz), such as wagon wheel or corkscrew
Lean fully cooked ham, cut into bite-size strips (6 oz)
Small Red or green sweet pepper, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. If using fresh asparagus, snap off and discard the woody bases.
2. Bias-slice the asparagus into 1-inch pieces. Or thaw and drain frozen asparagus.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine stewed tomatoes, parsley, basil, oregano, and ground red pepper (if desired). Bring to boil.
4. Simmer the sauce uncovered for about 15 minutes or until reduced to 2-1/2 cups, stirring occasionally.
5. Add evaporated milk all at once and stir constantly. Heat mixture through; do not boil.
6. Prepare pasta according to package directions, adding asparagus, ham, and sweet pepper to boiling water during the last 4 minutes of cooking time.
7. Drain pasta and vegetables.
8. Place pasta mixture on a serving platter. Spoon sauce over pasta. Serve with Parmesan cheese if desired. Serve immediately.
9. Makes 4 or 5 main-dish servings.
